about.text: We make each of our products the way they have always been made, with time, care and carefully selected ingredients.

services.title: What can we offer you?
services.text: Handcrafted products, cosy breaks, unforgettable moments…

producto.title: Our products | Panadería Ana
producto.description: Empanadas, pastries, cakes and artisan coffee at Panadería Ana, Cee.

sections.title: The taste of Galician tradition
sections.body: Our homemade empanadas are prepared with thin, crispy dough and fillings made with local produce. You can choose from scallops, octopus, cod, meat, tuna or even apple for a sweet touch. Each one reflects the authentic flavour of the Costa da Morte.

sections.title: Tasty, practical and always homemade
sections.body: Perfect to take away or enjoy on the spot, our empanadillas are prepared with the same homemade dough as our large empanadas. Available in classic flavours such as ham and cheese, meat and tuna. Small in size, big in flavour.

sections.title: Freshly made every morning
sections.body: We prepare daily croissants, napolitanas, ensaimadas and a wide variety of puff pastry sweets. All made with quality butter and a touch of love, ideal to accompany our coffees or take home.

sections.title: We sweeten your most special moments
sections.body: We make homemade sponge cakes and custom cakes for birthdays, celebrations or any special occasion. You can choose flavours, fillings and decoration to your liking. Each cake is handcrafted with fresh, natural ingredients.

sections.title: Little delights for any moment
sections.body: From cream pastries to millefeuille or fruit tartlets, at Panadería Ana we make a wide variety of sweets with traditional recipes. Perfect to accompany your coffee or enjoy during a relaxed afternoon in Cee.

sections.title: Your moment to unwind
sections.body: In addition to our baked goods, we serve freshly brewed coffee to enjoy in our shop. An ideal option to share a pleasant moment.

servicios.title: Our services | Panadería Ana
servicios.description: Quality products, custom orders, coffee shop and wholesale at Panadería Ana, Cee.

quality.title: Bread and sweets made with love
quality.text: Since our very first batches, quality has been our priority. We use carefully selected ingredients and traditional techniques that guarantee a unique flavour and texture.

personalized.title: Create something unique for your special occasion
personalized.text: Made-to-order items for celebrations, birthdays and events. Personalise your cake, choose flavours, fillings and decoration.
personalized.point: Talk to us and we'll design your idea together.

cafeteria.title: Your corner to unwind
cafeteria.text: Aromas, flavour and tranquillity. Have a freshly brewed coffee accompanied by our sweet or savoury specialties.
cafeteria.quote: Coffee is the best excuse to get together.

wholesale.title: Partner with us
wholesale.text: We supply bars, restaurants and supermarkets in the area with our artisan products.
wholesale.point: Join those who already trust our bakehouse.

cta.title: Want to know more or place a special order?
cta.text: Call us or visit us, we'll be happy to help.

nosotros.title: About Us | Panadería Ana
nosotros.description: Discover the story of Panadería Ana, an artisan bakery and pastry shop with roots in Mazaricos and its heart in Cee.

history.title: A story kneaded with love and roots
history.body:
Panadería Ana was born in 2011, with the dream of bringing the taste of authenticity to every home. Our first steps began in A Picota, a small corner where, with our own oven and tireless hands, we shaped what we are today: a family united by the love of bread and artisan pastry.

Eleven years ago, in 2014, we opened our shop in Cee, a town that welcomed us warmly from day one. Here we found our home and here we remain, with the same determination as always: to make every bread, every empanada and every sweet as if it were the first.

This year marked a very special moment: we moved our bakehouse from Mazaricos directly to the shop in Cee. We no longer just bake for the town, but we do it from the heart of the town. Every aroma that comes from the oven is a small story shared with our neighbours.

philosophy.title: Made in-house, from start to finish
philosophy.body:
At Panadería Ana we believe in what is made with hands and heart. Our products are the result of daily work, of recipes passed down from generation to generation, and of dedication to every detail. There is no rush in our bakehouse: every dough needs its time, and we give it just that.

We like to think that bread doesn't just nourish, it also brings people together. That's why, every day, we light the oven with the excitement of sharing a little piece of our story with those who walk through the door.

cee.title: Cee: our home, our inspiration
cee.text: Cee is not just the place where we are, it is part of who we are. Here we have grown, formed friendships and shared thousands of stories around bread. Every customer who walks through the door reminds us why we started and why we carry on: because here, in every loaf, in every sweet, there is a little piece of our town.
cee.quote: Blessed is he to whom heaven gave a piece of bread without laying on him the obligation of thanking anyone but heaven itself.

contacto.title: Contact | Panadería Ana
contacto.description: Get in touch with Panadería Ana in Cee: phone, WhatsApp, opening hours and location.

intro.title: If you have any enquiry, please contact us
intro.text: We are available at our shop in Cee, by phone, or via WhatsApp. Don't hesitate to get in touch!

dev.title: Do you like our website?
dev.text: If you have any questions about the website or would like a similar one for your business, feel free to contact the developer.

tartadequeso.title: Cheesecake | Panadería Ana
tartadequeso.description: Discover the famous cheesecake at Panadería Ana in Cee: creamy, artisan and with an unmistakable Galician touch.

quote.text: A dessert without cheese is like a beauty with only one eye.
quote.author: Jean Anthelme Brillat-Savarin

history.title: A story of flavour, from the heart of Galicia
history.caption: Roman mosaic depicting bakers making a cheesecake in Pompeii, circa 70 AD.
history.body:
Although cheesecake has ancient roots (the Greeks already offered cheesecakes to Olympic athletes), in Galicia it found its own character. The combination of local cheeses, fresh eggs and artisan care gave it a unique identity.

At Panadería Ana we continue that tradition: we bake each cake as if it were the first, caring for the creamy texture and balanced flavour that so defines us.

flavors.headTitle: Four versions to fall in love with
flavors.title: A modern touch
flavors.body:
At Panadería Ana we believe that tradition is not at odds with innovation. Every cake is born from a homemade recipe that we care for lovingly: fresh ingredients, patient baking and that creamy texture that defines us. From that authentic base, we go a step further to surprise those looking for something special.

That is why we have created our special cheesecakes: Lotus, with its spiced sweetness; Pistachio, delicate and creamy; Kinder, for chocolate lovers; and Red Berries, fresh and fruity. A modern touch that respects the essence of homemade cheesecake and celebrates our passion for baking.

stats.title: Did you know…?
stats.text: Last summer our cheesecake won over hundreds of people in Cee. And the numbers speak for themselves!

items.label:
Whole cakes sold
throughout the entire summer

cta.title: But it's not our only homemade Galician cake…
cta.label: View Galician Cake

tartadesantiago.title: Tarta de Santiago | Panadería Ana
tartadesantiago.description: Discover the authentic Tarta de Santiago at Panadería Ana: history, tradition and flavour with 100% Galician almond.

history.title: A recipe born along the Camino
history.caption: Pedro Portocarrero, bishop and cleric.
history.body:
The first historical record of the tarta de Santiago is from the bishop Pedro Portocarrero, when he first visited the University of Santiago in 1577. Ever since, it became a symbol of the city. The cake travelled in the saddlebags of pilgrims along the Camino and it soon became a symbol of Galician hospitality. Its recipe was already well documented by the 16th century.

What is extraordinary is its simplicity: ground almond, sugar and egg. Nothing more. No wheat flour, no preservatives. And here at Panadería Ana we go a step further: we use 100% natural Galician almond flour, which enhances its original flavour and its rich, moist texture.

making.headSub: The original recipe needs no enhancements: only good almond, sugar and fresh eggs.
making.videoAlt: Artisan elaboration of the Tarta de Santiago
making.subtitle: Our way of baking
making.body:
What truly makes our product unique is our absolute respect for the traditional recipe. We make our cakes exclusively with the finest quality ground almonds, without resorting to added flours or ingredients that cut costs at the expense of flavour. Every bite preserves the authenticity of centuries of Galician baking tradition.

In a world where industrial production prioritises speed over quality, we choose the opposite path: craftsmanship. Each cake is handmade with patience and care, maintaining the perfect balance of texture, aroma and flavour. This is how we guarantee a unique experience that transports you directly to the deepest roots of Galicia.

Many pilgrims and tourists passing through Cee look for an authentic souvenir, a piece of Galicia they can take home with them. Our Tarta de Santiago is much more than a dessert: it is a symbol, a testament to tradition, hospitality and dedication.

That is why we put all our care and effort into every preparation. We carefully select our raw ingredients, oversee every stage of the process, and pay attention to every detail, from the batter to the final packaging. We want you, when you taste it, to enjoy not only its unmistakable flavour but also the history and soul it carries.

Whether as a gift, a keepsake, or simply a sweet treat, our Tarta de Santiago is an invitation to savour Galicia with your heart.

mazaricos.title: Our creation: Tarta de Mazaricos
mazaricos.body: A special version of the Tarta de Santiago, with high quality nuts and a touch of local honey. A homage to our country origins in A Picota and to nature itself.

stats.title: Did you know…?
stats.text: Our Tarta de Santiago has travelled far. Tourists and pilgrims have taken them home to England, Germany, America, Korea… Where will you take it?

cta.title: Want to know how we got here?
cta.label: View our story

larpeira.title: Larpeira | Panadería Ana
larpeira.description: Discover the authentic Larpeira at Panadería Ana: Galician tradition in every slice of our artisan bread.

quote.text:
Non é xa o cativo currunchiño
que din escolas vellas:
Galicia é infinda!
quote.author: Florencio Delgado Gurriarán

history.title: A sweet rooted in Galicia
history.body:
The Galician larpeira was born from the ancient sweet buns made with butter and anise in different areas of Galicia. Over time, the pastry cream and anise syrup that make it so special today were gradually added.

It is said that around 1927, in a pastry shop in Cambre, the famous diamond-shaped cuts began to be made in the dough — a hallmark that gives it its unique appearance and helps distribute the cream more evenly. Since then, this dessert has become a true classic at our celebrations.

galicia.title: The sweet bun of Galician celebration
galicia.caption: The artisanal Larpeira, a symbol of Galician baking.
galicia.body:
The Larpeira is one of the sweetest treasures of traditional Galician pastry. Originally from rural areas, this sweet bread was traditionally made for special celebrations, patron saint festivals, and important family events.

Its name comes from the Galician word «larpeira», referring to its fluffy texture and slightly sweet flavour. What sets our Larpeira apart is the use of local butter and free-range eggs, which give it that characteristic golden colour and a tender, aromatic crumb.

At Panadería Ana we respect the traditional recipe but add our personal touch: a subtle hint of anisette that enhances its aroma without altering its traditional essence.

sweet.title: A sweet touch
sweet.body: A special version of our traditional Larpeira, with a delicious fresh whipped cream filling that adds a smooth texture and an irresistible flavour. Perfect for celebrations, afternoon snacks, or simply enjoying a sweet Galician moment.

stats.title: Did you know?
stats.text: The Galician Larpeira is one of the most typical sweets of Noite de San Xoán (St. John's Eve), when bonfires light up the beaches and squares of Galicia every June 23rd.

cta.title: Fancy enjoying a freshly made Larpeira?
cta.label: Place your order

tartagallega.title: Galician Cake | Panadería Ana
tartagallega.description: Discover the Galician Cake at Panadería Ana: a unique fusion of traditional almond cake with Galician semi-cured cheese.

quote.text:
¡Nin rosiñas brancas, nin claveles roxos!
Eu venero as froliñas dos toxos.
quote.author: Antonio Noriega Varela, As froliñas dos toxos

history.title: Galician essence in every bite
history.caption: Our Galician Cake: the perfect mix of tradition and innovation.
history.body:
Our Galician Cake is our most beloved creation, a fusion of everything that celebrates Galician baking tradition: almond cake, with its century-long history, and delicious semi-cured Galician cheese, with its unique flavour.

Here at Panadería Ana we wanted to bring together two of our specialties in a dessert that represents our native land: a rustic almond base combined with the sweetness of creamy cheese.

harmony.headSub: The perfect balance between almond cake and Galician cheese.
harmony.title: A contrast of flavours and textures
harmony.caption: The perfect contrast: almond cake base and artisanal Galician semi-cured cheese.
harmony.body:
Our Galician Cake starts with a base of traditional almond cake, made from the best-quality almonds crushed to a flour, mixed with fresh eggs and our own special touch that we have mastered through the years. This base provides a moist, rich texture that hydrates beautifully with the cheese.

On top of this base we add a layer of cheesecake made with country cheeses, including semi-cured Galician cheese that rises during baking, creating a unique, mousse-like fluffiness. The result is a sublime contrast: the sweetness of the almond with the savoury note of the cheese, the dense texture of the cake with the airiness of the cheesecake.

tradition.title: A homage to our roots
tradition.body:
While our modern cheesecakes (Lotus, Pistachio, Kinder and Red Berries) explore new combinations of flavours, the Galician Cake is our tribute to all that is authentically Galician.

This creation does not follow international trends; instead it is rooted in what is ours: the almond of the Galician woods and the cheese of our cows, raised on the green pastures of our land. It is the answer for those who seek the taste of Galicia in its purest form.

points.title: Flavourful almond base
points.text: Made from the same exquisite almonds as our Tarta de Santiago, creating a moist, rich texture that hydrates perfectly with the layer of cheese.

points.title: Semi-cured local cheese
points.text: Galician semi-cured farmhouse cheese, giving a more intense flavour and an experience no cheese lover should miss.

panchocolatenaranja.title: Chocolate & orange bread | Panadería Ana
panchocolatenaranja.description: Discover our chocolate and orange bread: an artisan dough with cocoa and pieces of sweet orange, baked in the Panadería Ana workshop.

history.title: Cocoa and orange in every slice
history.body:
Our chocolate and orange bread was born from the love of pairing two flavours that understand each other perfectly: pure cocoa, kneaded right into the dough, and pieces of sweet orange scattered all through the crumb.

It's neither a sponge cake nor a pastry: it's real bread, made with the same dough and the same care as all of our other loaves. With a crisp crust and a tender crumb, the cocoa lends colour and aroma and the orange leaves that fresh, fruity note that surprises you in every bite.

craft.title: The same dough, the same care
craft.videoAlt: Making the chocolate and orange bread
craft.caption: We knead the cocoa into the dough and shape every loaf by hand.
craft.body:
The chocolate and orange bread is made exactly like all of our other breads: with the same ingredients and the same long cold fermentation that develops the flavour, improves keeping and gives a lighter, more digestible crumb.

The cocoa goes in during kneading so it colours the dough completely, and the orange pieces are added at the end, by hand, so they stay whole and evenly spread. The same quality bread as always, just with an indulgent twist.

orange.headTitle: The balance between cocoa and orange
orange.headSub: Intense yet never cloying: bread for breakfast, an afternoon snack, or with coffee.
orange.title: Intense, fruity and handcrafted
orange.imgAlt: Close-up of the chocolate and orange bread crumb
orange.caption: A tender, airy crumb with the colour of cocoa.
orange.body: The secret is in the contrast: the gentle bitterness of the cocoa is balanced by the citrus sweetness of the orange, without one overpowering the other. The result is an indulgent yet light bread, perfect for dunking in a coffee or enjoying just as it is.

points.title: Cocoa in the dough
points.text: Cocoa kneaded right into the bread, giving it colour, aroma and an intense yet balanced flavour.

points.title: Sweet orange
points.text: Pieces of orange folded in by hand that bring freshness and a citrus note to every slice.

sweet.title: The best way to enjoy it
sweet.body: It's delicious freshly baked, but it's even better lightly toasted with a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil, which lifts the aroma of the orange and the cocoa. Ideal for breakfast, an afternoon snack, or alongside a good coffee.

stats.title: Did you know…?
stats.text: The pairing of chocolate and orange is one of the great classics of baking: the citrus lifts the cocoa and softens its bitterness, which is why they work so well together.

cta.title: Fancy trying our chocolate and orange bread?
cta.label: Place your order
